contribution.title: Intrinsic curvature (Theorema Egregium)
contribution.description: Showed that the Gaussian curvature of a surface is an intrinsic invariant, independent of embedding.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Foundation for the modern notion of curvature in Riemannian geometry, which GR inherits.

contribution.title: Method of least squares
contribution.description: Statistical estimation method for orbit determination and other inverse problems.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Useful for fitting model parameters to observational data (EHT, gravitational wave catalogs).

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Compute carefully and exhaustively; check special cases against the general result.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is intrinsic to this object, independent of how we describe it?
What does a careful asymptotic expansion say?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Famously slow to publish, so others sometimes got the credit.

reference.citation: Gauss, C. F. (1828). Disquisitiones generales circa superficies curvas. Commentationes Soc. Reg. Sci. Gott. Recent. 6, 99.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Anything involving intrinsic curvature, coordinate-independent geometric quantities, or parameter estimation.
how_to_apply.cautions: Modern conventions differ from Gauss's; translate carefully.
